description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof covered in reddish-brown, textured tiles that resemble traditional clay or terracotta. The facade is characterized by a timber-framed design with white infill panels, evoking a classic half-timbered architectural style. Two small dormer windows protrude from the roof, each with its own miniature gable and white-framed window, adding visual interest and natural light to the upper level. The main structure is rectangular, with dark wooden beams outlining the walls and supporting the roof. The lower portion of the house includes a wooden door and window, both framed by the same dark timber. The overall appearance suggests a quaint, historic European cottage, likely found in a medieval or old-town urban setting.
description: A quaint, half-timbered house with a steep gable roof of reddish-brown tiles, featuring dormer windows and timber-framed white walls. The design evokes a classic European cottage or medieval town home.
description_view_0: The roof is fully visible, showing its reddish-brown, textured tiles and the overhanging eaves.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the steep gable end, timber framing, and a small window beneath the roof peak.
description_view_2: The front view displays two dormer windows, the main door, and symmetrical timber framing on the facade.
description_view_3: The opposite gable end is visible, with timber framing and a small window, mirroring the other side.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door with timber framing, no porch or steps, and minimal decorative elements
